articles.title: Trump’s Global Tariffs: What’s the Latest on the Trade War
articles.description: The president has unveiled a complicated system to forge ahead with his trade war, after the Supreme Court struck down his original levies.

articles.title: Iran War and Trump’s Tariffs Threaten a Resilient U.S. Economy
articles.description: As renewed fighting with Iran sends oil and gas prices soaring, President Trump has imposed new global tariffs that could push up prices further.

articles.description: Households and businesses were already contending with an affordability crunch. A new round of tariffs could inflict more pain.

articles.title: Brent Crude Oil Prices Top $100 as Conflict With Iran Drags On
articles.description: Stocks in Asia were down following a sell-off in the U.S. markets over soaring oil prices, rising borrowing costs and worries about the technology industry.

articles.title: As Canada Opens Gordie Howe International Bridge, Americans Are Told to Stay Home
articles.description: After a delay ordered by President Trump and new tariffs against Canada, the bridge between Windsor, Ontario, and Detroit opens in “underwhelming” fashion on Friday.

articles.title: U.S. to see higher generic drug prices thanks to tariffs, CEO of leading India pharma firm warns
articles.description: CEO of Indian pharma company Dr Reddy's has warned that Trump's proposed tariffs on generic drugs will make them more expensive for patients.

articles.title: Pentagon lowers official Iran war death toll, omitting four killed this month
articles.description: The Pentagon lowered its official Iran war death toll to 14 from 18, removing four service members killed this month.

articles.title: South Korea A.I. Billionaire Ordered to Pay More Than Half a Billion Dollars in ‘Divorce of the Century’
articles.description: A South Korean court awarded $644 million to Roh Soh-yeong, who had sought much more from her ex-husband, Chey Tae-won, the head of the conglomerate behind SK Hynix.

articles.title: Bangladesh’s President Resigns Amid Uncertainty Over Ex-Leader’s Plans
articles.description: President Mohammed Shahabuddin stepped down as speculation swirled over the possible return of his onetime ally Sheikh Hasina, who was ousted as prime minister and fled to India.

articles.title: Russia and Ukraine Trade Blows With Missiles and Drones
articles.description: Ukrainian strikes set fire to warehouses and other sites in Russia, and Russian bombing rocked Ukraine’s capital, as the two countries escalated a deadly air campaign.

articles.title: 5 Killed in West Bank Shooting, Officials Say, Among the Deadliest in Months
articles.description: The authorities said four Palestinians and an Israeli were killed in the incident. The details of events remain unclear, with officials offering differing accounts.

articles.title: Man in a Spider-Man Costume Rides to the Rescue in Arkansas
articles.description: Was that Peter Parker helping a man in a wheelchair cross an intersection? No, it was Chris Hellenthal, a college student returning from a summer job.

related.title: Good Samaritan in Spider-Man costume helps man in wheelchair cross busy intersection in viral moment

articles.description: At 71, Debra Winger and Arliss Howard have aged into a married-couples acting tradition that Jessica Tandy and Hume Cronyn began.

articles.title: Venezuela’s Earthquakes Show Wealth Divide, Even in Death

articles.description: A French government delegation will travel to its southern neighbor to learn how it copes with long spells of hot weather.

articles.title: Fetterman trolls the Democratic Socialists of America as Israel kills Hamas terrorists: 'Cry harder'
articles.description: Sen. John Fetterman tagged the Democratic Socialists of America on X, telling them to "cry harder" as the Israel Defense Forces highlighted the elimination of several terrorists.

articles.description: Lee was highlighting the country's excessive investment in real estate, and highlighted that Japan's housing market "burst like a balloon."
